title: How to prevent text dissappearing inside of point brackets "< > "
excerpt: We often copy and paste legal names for trusts into our boards but we have noticed that anything inside of a pointy bracket  which is the main way to designate a trust in Australia will remain on the board while you are …

posts.author: Moeydc (Jon Moses)
posts.cookedHtml:
We often copy and paste legal names for trusts into our boards but we have noticed that anything inside of a pointy bracket which is the main way to designate a trust in Australia will remain on the board while you are working on it, But as soon as you leave the board and come back, everything inside of these is gone.

is there an easy fix for this please?

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Moeydc

I believe what’s happening here is that monday.com reads anything inside < > as HTML code. While you’re editing, it can look fine but once the board refreshes or you leave and come back, the system strips out what it thinks is code. That’s why the text inside the angle brackets disappears.

Unfortunately, there isn’t a setting to turn this behaviour off.

The simplest workaround is to avoid using < > and switch to something like () or [] instead. I know that’s not ideal given how trusts are commonly formatted in Australia, but it’s the most reliable fix right now.

posts.author: jofra886archer (jofra archer)
posts.cookedHtml: I know that especially with legal docs where every character counts.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Finally! Someone else found this quirk.

I add a space between the < and the text. So < Trust Name >.

You could prompt Sidekick to add a space after < and before > automatically for you.

posts.author: Moeydc (Jon Moses)
posts.cookedHtml: @YOGESHP @farah1 @ProjectMastermind thankyou all for the amazing recommendations, it is very much appreciated
